subtopics.title: Definition and Importance of Technical Drawing
common_misconceptions: CAD has replaced manual drawing completely
subtopics.teaching_note: Technical drawing = precise, detailed graphic representation of objects using standard symbols and conventions. Used by engineers, architects, builders, manufacturers. Standards: ISO (International), ANSI (American), BS (British). Nigerian standard: adopt ISO/BS. Careers: mechanical engineer, civil engineer, architect, interior designer, industrial designer. Software: AutoCAD, SolidWorks, ArchiCAD, SketchUp.

subtopics.title: Drawing Instruments
common_misconceptions: Any pencil can be used for technical drawing (use 2H, H, HB grades)
Drawing board is just a table
subtopics.teaching_note: Standard instruments: Drawing board (A0, A1, A2, A3 sizes). T-square: for horizontal lines. Set squares: 45° and 30°-60° triangles, used together for various angles. Pencil grades: 6H (hardest, lightest) to 6B (softest, darkest). Technical drawing: use 2H for construction lines, H for outlines, HB for lettering. Compass: for circles and arcs. Divider: for transferring measurements. Paper sizes: A0 (841×1189mm), A1 (594×841mm), A2 (420×594mm), A3 (297×420mm), A4 (210×297mm).

subtopics.title: Basic Geometrical Constructions
common_misconceptions: Construction lines should be dark like outline lines
Estimation is acceptable in technical drawing
All angles can be drawn with just a protractor
subtopics.teaching_note: Key constructions: 1) Bisect a line. 2) Bisect an angle. 3) Draw perpendicular from point to line. 4) Draw parallel lines. 5) Divide a line into equal parts. 6) Construct polygons (triangle, square, pentagon, hexagon). 7) Draw tangents to circles. Construction lines should be LIGHT (2H pencil). Final lines DARKER (H or HB). NEVER estimate - use compass and ruler for precision.
